content.thinking: Let me inspect the current configuration of the spark-pi namespace and then prepare a migration/upgrade plan.

content.text: I'll inspect the current spark-pi namespace configuration first, then prepare the migration plan.

input.command: kubectl get deploy spark-master spark-worker -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1
input.description: Get deployment configs for spark-master and spark-worker
Get job configurations in spark-pi namespace

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
items:
- apiVersion: batch/v1
  kind: Job
  metadata:
    annotations:
      kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
        {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-pi","namespace":"spark-pi"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-pi","component":"driver"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"==============================================\"\necho \"  SparkPi Job\"\necho \"==============================================\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[2] \\\n  --driver-memory 512m \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  1000\nEXIT_CODE=$?\necho \"SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE\"\nexit $EXIT_CODE\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-submit","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark-pi"}}}}
    creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T21:31:39Z"
    generation: 1
    labels:
      app: spark-pi
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
      batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
      component: driver
      controller-uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
      job-name: spark-pi
    name: spark-pi
    namespace: spark-pi
    resourceVersion: "908954"
    uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
  spec:
    backoffLimit: 0
    completionMode: NonIndexed
    completions: 1
    manualSelector: false
    parallelism: 1
    podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
    suspend: false
    template:
      metadata:
        creationTimestamp: null
        labels:
          app: spark-pi
          batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
          batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-pi
          component: driver
          controller-uid: 386625b9-c4d9-4a09-940c-b880e8ccec1c
          job-name: spark-pi
      spec:
        containers:
        - command:
          - /bin/bash
          - -c
          - |
            echo "=============================================="
            echo "  SparkPi Job"
            echo "=============================================="
            export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
            /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
              --master local[2] \
              --driver-memory 512m \
              --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
              /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
              1000
            EXIT_CODE=$?
            echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
            exit $EXIT_CODE
          image: apache/spark:3.5.3
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          name: spark-submit
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            requests:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 512Mi
          terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
          terminationMessagePolicy: File
        dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
        restartPolicy: Never
        schedulerName: default-scheduler
        securityContext: {}
        serviceAccount: spark-pi
        serviceAccountName: spark-pi
        terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
  status:
    completionTime: "2026-06-23T21:33:57Z"
    conditions:
    - lastProbeTime: "2026-06-23T21:33:57Z"
      lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-23T21:33:57Z"
      message: Reached expected number of succeeded pods
      reason: CompletionsReached
      status: "True"
      type: SuccessCriteriaMet
    - lastProbeTime: "2026-06-23T21:33:59Z"
      lastTransitionTime: "2026-06-23T21:33:59Z"
      message: Reached expected number of succeeded pods
      reason: CompletionsReached
      status: "True"
      type: Complete
    ready: 0
    startTime: "2026-06-23T21:31:40Z"
    succeeded: 1
    terminating: 0
    uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

metadata:

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  findings.txt: |-
    SPARK-PI NAMESPACE COMPLIANCE AUDIT
    Date: 2026-06-23
    Auditor: Automated Compliance Scan (read-only)
    ============================================================

    == COMPLIANT ITEMS ==

    [PASS] RBAC-1: Dedicated service account
      A dedicated 'spark-pi' service account is used by all workloads
      (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-pi job). The 'default' service
      account is not used for any workload.

    [PASS] RBAC-2: Namespace-scoped role (not ClusterRole)
      'spark-pi-role' is a Role (not ClusterRole), limiting RBAC permissions
      to the spark-pi namespace only. Blast radius is namespace-contained.

    [PASS] RBAC-3: RoleBinding correctly targets dedicated SA
      'spark-pi-role-binding' binds 'spark-pi-role' to the 'spark-pi'
      service account in the correct namespace.

    [PASS] RESOURCES-1: CPU/memory requests and limits set on all containers
      All three workloads (spark-master, spark-worker, spark-submit) have:
        requests: cpu=200m, memory=512Mi
        limits:   cpu=500m, memory=1Gi
      Prevents unbounded resource consumption.

    [PASS] IMAGE-1: Versioned image tag (not 'latest')
      All containers use 'apache/spark:3.5.3'. The ':latest' anti-pattern
      is avoided, ensuring reproducible deployments.

    [PASS] IMAGE-2: imagePullPolicy set to IfNotPresent
      All containers use imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent, preventing
      unnecessary re-pulls and enforcing image stability at runtime.

    [PASS] JOB-1: backoffLimit=0 on spark-pi job
      The spark-pi batch job has backoffLimit=0, preventing automatic
      retries on failure and avoiding runaway job costs.

    [PASS] JOB-2: Job completed successfully
      The spark-pi job completed (status: Complete, succeeded=1,
      completionTime: 2026-06-23T21:33:57Z).

    ============================================================

    == NON-COMPLIANT ITEMS / GAPS ==

    [FAIL] SEC-1 [CRITICAL]: No pod or container security context on any workload
      All three workloads (spark-master Deployment, spark-worker Deployment,
      spark-pi Job) have securityContext: {} at both pod and container level.
      Missing controls:
        - runAsNonRoot: true  (containers likely run as root)
        - runAsUser / runAsGroup  (no UID/GID pinning)
        - allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
        - readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
        - capabilities.drop: [ALL]
      Risk: containers with root access can escalate privileges and access
      sensitive host resources if container isolation is breached.
      Remediation: add a securityContext block to each pod and container spec.

    [FAIL] RBAC-4 [HIGH]: 'pods' resource added to role beyond original declaration
      The live 'spark-pi-role' grants create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch
      on 'pods' in addition to services, configmaps, and persistentvolumeclaims.
      The kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration annotation shows the
      role was originally created WITHOUT the 'pods' resource. The 'pods' entry
      was added post-creation, representing an undeclared permission expansion.
      For a SparkPi job running in local[2] mode (no dynamic executor pod
      allocation), pod CRUD permissions are unnecessary.
      Risk: the spark-pi SA can create, delete, or modify pods in this namespace,
      enabling potential lateral movement or workload manipulation.
      Remediation: remove 'pods' from the role's resource list.

    [FAIL] NET-1 [HIGH]: No NetworkPolicy defined
      The spark-pi namespace has zero NetworkPolicy objects. All pod-to-pod
      traffic within the namespace and from other namespaces is unrestricted.
      The Spark master WebUI (port 8080) and Spark protocol port (7077) are
      reachable by any pod in the cluster.
      Risk: unrestricted lateral movement; any compromised pod in the cluster
      can connect to Spark master/worker endpoints.
      Remediation: create NetworkPolicy resources to restrict ingress/egress
      to only required ports and sources.

    [FAIL] QUOTA-1 [MEDIUM]: No LimitRange or ResourceQuota in namespace
      No LimitRange or ResourceQuota objects exist in spark-pi. Resource
      limits on current workloads are set voluntarily; future workloads
      (or a modified manifest) could omit limits and run unconstrained.
      Risk: noisy-neighbor resource exhaustion affecting the cluster.
      Remediation: add a LimitRange with default limits and a ResourceQuota
      to cap total namespace consumption.

    [FAIL] IMAGE-3 [MEDIUM]: Image not pinned to immutable digest
      'apache/spark:3.5.3' is a mutable tag. A registry push could change
      what this tag resolves to without changing the manifest.
      Risk: supply-chain substitution; undetected image replacement.
      Remediation: pin images to 'apache/spark@sha256:<digest>' for
      immutable, verifiable provenance.

    [FAIL] RBAC-5 [LOW]: 'default' service account lacks automountServiceAccountToken=false
      The 'default' service account in spark-pi does not set
      automountServiceAccountToken: false. Any pod that omits a
      serviceAccountName (e.g. a misconfigured or injected workload) will
      automatically mount a 'default' SA token.
      Risk: unintended API access from pods that should have no cluster access.
      Remediation: patch the 'default' SA with automountServiceAccountToken: false.

    ============================================================

    == SUMMARY ==

    Total checks: 14
      Passed (compliant):    8
      Failed (gaps/issues):  6
        Critical: 1 (SEC-1 — no security context)
        High:     2 (RBAC-4 pod permissions drift, NET-1 no NetworkPolicy)
        Medium:   2 (QUOTA-1 no LimitRange/Quota, IMAGE-3 mutable tag)
        Low:      1 (RBAC-5 default SA token automount)

    NOTE: This is a read-only audit. No changes were made to the cluster.
    Remediation should be planned and applied separately after review.
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T21:35:28Z"
  name: config-audit
  namespace: spark-pi
  resourceVersion: "909053"
  uid: 02f854c2-c711-4c4e-b780-2d99b442c6f7

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive picture of the spark-pi namespace. Let me analyze what I found:

**Current Configuration:**

1. **Deployments:**
   - `spark-master`: image `apache/spark:3.5.3`, 1 replica, resources: requests cpu=200m/mem=512Mi, limits cpu=500m/mem=1Gi, securityContext={}
   - `spark-worker`: image `apache/spark:3.5.3`, 1 replica, resources same as above, env SPARK_WORKER_MEMORY=1G, SPARK_WORKER_CORES=1, securityContext={}
2. **Job:**
   - `spark-pi`: image `apache/spark:3.5.3`, backoffLimit=0, driver-memory=512m, --master local[2], 1000 iterations, resources same as above, securityContext={}
3. **RBAC:**
   - Role `spark-pi-role`: grants create/get/list/watch/delete/update/patch on services, configmaps, persistentvolumeclaims, and pods (pods was added post-creation - drift)
   - RoleBinding `spark-pi-role-binding`: binds to `spark-pi`
4. **ServiceAccounts:**
   - `spark-pi`: used by all workloads
   - `default`: no automountServiceAccountToken=false
5. **Services:**
   - `spark-master`: ClusterIP, ports 7077 (spark) and 8080 (webui)
6. **ConfigMaps:**
   - `config-audit`: already documents audit findings
7. **Security gaps:**
   - No pod/container securityContext on any workload
   - No NetworkPolicy, LimitRange, or ResourceQuota
   - Images use mutable tags instead of pinned digests

The config-audit ConfigMap already has the compliance issues documented, which gives me a solid foundation. Now I'm planning a comprehensive migration strategy and will save it as a ConfigMap named `change-plan`, starting with the priority order for implementation.

I'm ordering the changes by criticality: first addressing security contexts and RBAC privilege drift, then adding network policies and resource controls, followed by image pinning and finally the default ServiceAccount configuration. I'll format this as a structured plan document and create it as a ConfigMap using shell-safe string handling.

content.text: I have all the information I need. There's also a pre-existing audit in `config-audit` that I'll incorporate. Now I'll compose the plan and create the ConfigMap.
